title: Core and Pilot Mechanics in NME Same as NGE
display_title: Core and Pilot Mechanics in NME Same as NGE
wikitext:
[]: Core and Pilot Mechanics in NME: Same as NGE?             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Fri Jul 19, 2013 11:12 am [Split from 3.33 Things You Noticed . - Monk] Yaywalter wrote: I was reading the 3.33 faq this evening, and an idea hit me when I read the speculation on the possibility that he isn't Wille's first attempt to recover Shinji due to his foot being labeled "BM-03": How many people were in Unit 01? Three: Shinji Ikari, Rei Ayanami, and Yui Ikari. If, during the recovery process, they were able to recovere all three (BM-01,-02, and -03) from the Eva, that would explain why Shinji now has a 0% sync ratio with it. Though it does leave us to wonder why Misato and Ritsuko would lie to Shinji, so I don't put too much stock in my theory personally. But I thought it was an interesting possibility nonetheless.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Fri Jul 19, 2013 1:11 pm TruthfulLie wrote: This assumes that soul and core mechanics of NGE still applies to NTE, but we can't be certain as Asuka was shown testing unit 03 and Mari piloting 02 by switching the cores.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 19, 2013 3:34 pm Wait, it's never said that Mari switched the core for piloting EVA-02, Mari is supposed to be the pilot of EVA-05, if a core was exclusive of a pilot, then how could she swap hers with the one of EVA-02 since EVA-05 blew-up at the start of 2.0? And every pilot was seen getting into more than one Eva at some point of the films : Rei piloted EVA-00 and Mark.09, Kaworu Mark.06 and EVA-13, Shinji EVA-01 and 13, Asuka EVA-02 and 03(at least the startup sequence), and Mari get the highest score with three different Evas piloted : 05, 02 and 08! I think that clearly means that in NTE, everyone who has the ability to pilot can pilot any Evangelion, maybe the synch rate is different when it's an core not specifically built for a pilot(although Mari didn't looked to have any problems with EVA-02), with the probable exception of EVA-01, because Yui is picky about who can pilot her. As for the EVA-03 incident, I doubt Gendo planned it to happen, and even less that he intended EVA-03 to be destroyed : with that and the previous "accident" of EVA-04, he ended deprived of of two last generation Evangelion and a fully trained pilot, and SEELE already understood Gendo's plans and were taking actions against him(EVA-04, and maybe even EVA-03), and were also very near to sent Mark.06.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Jul 19, 2013 4:36 pm We don't have confirmation the cores were actually swapped. They just give us enough to make it probable: -Cores are removable -The machine that removes it is called "Evangelion Custom Core Extraction Device" Things however get improbable because as mentioned: -Mari's 05 core should have went boom. -If Asuka's was transplanted to 03 it's also gone. -When Mari boards 02 all we're told is "Rewriting Access Key", nothing about the actual core. For now it's safest to assume Yui keeps Eva-01 secure and every other Eva is like the village bicycle.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Fri Jul 19, 2013 5:23 pm ElMariachi wrote: Wait, it's never said that Mari switched the core for piloting EVA-02, Mari is supposed to be the pilot of EVA-05, if a core was exclusive of a pilot, then how could she swap hers with the one of EVA-02 since EVA-05 blew-up at the start of 2.0? And every pilot was seen getting into more than one Eva at some point of the films : Rei piloted EVA-00 and Mark.09, Kaworu Mark.06 and EVA-13, Shinji EVA-01 and 13, Asuka EVA-02 and 03(at least the startup sequence), and Mari get the highest score with three different Evas piloted : 05, 02 and 08! I think that clearly means that in NTE, everyone who has the ability to pilot can pilot any Evangelion, maybe the synch rate is different when it's an core not specifically built for a pilot(although Mari didn't looked to have any problems with EVA-02), with the probable exception of EVA-01, because Yui is picky about who can pilot her.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 19, 2013 6:33 pm There's an awful lot of handwaving going on here.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Fri Jul 19, 2013 9:10 pm Bagheera wrote: There's an awful lot of handwaving going on here.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Jul 19, 2013 11:38 pm It's handwaving to justify something that isn't in Evangelion anyway. The notion that Every. Single. Eva. has a mother's soul in it is absurd and leads to a bunch of fanwank about Nerv shoving a bunch of women into a soul suckatron or something and storing their souls somehow in case they need a pilot, even though such a program is never mentioned and that technology isn't even implied to exist. The reason the pilot candidates are all motherless is that having no mother makes it easier to synch with an Eva- they're open to the psychological dependence the Eva offers. It's not because there's a giant warehouse full of souls. There's a clear technological progression in the Evangelion program in the television series- the very first Eva has no safeties and kills the test pilot, the production model has some kind of safeguard but it doesn't work and the pilot just goes nuts and loses part of herself in the system, and the "prototype" and Rei are the first steps in developing an Eva that's piloted by a remote control system that uses a clone to fool the Eva into thinking there's a souls synching with it. The Mass Production Series do just that- it's stated clearly that Kaworu can control any Eva (except presumably Unit One) and the MPEs think that the Kaworu dummy plugs are Kaworu. They needed Toji because Nerv was barking up the wrong tree. The non-universality of "resident souls" is that much clearer in Rebuild. Asuka synchronized with Unit Three before Bardiel took over the Eva. Mari just hopped in Asuka's Eva and she was off. Mari pilots an Eva whose core cannot possibly have survived, and then pilots another one later. The destruction of the provisional Eva's core is absolute proof that while some Evas may have souls in them (I'm not convinced that Yui is in Shinji's Eva at all- there are clues pointing the other way), they don't have to. They're not required to function. Besides that, there's so many references to fandom theories and interpretations and winks to the viewer in these movies I can't see them incorporating the "resident soul" idea without some kind of nod or clue pointing to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 20, 2013 12:00 am Chuckman wrote: It's handwaving to justify something that isn't in Evangelion anyway. The notion that Every. Single. Eva. has a mother's soul in it is absurd and leads to a bunch of fanwank about Nerv shoving a bunch of women into a soul suckatron or something and storing their souls somehow in case they need a pilot, even though such a program is never mentioned and that technology isn't even implied to exist. The reason the pilot candidates are all motherless is that having no mother makes it easier to synch with an Eva- they're open to the psychological dependence the Eva offers. It's not because there's a giant warehouse full of souls.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 20, 2013 12:23 am Bagheera wrote: I know! Pay no attention to the fact that one of NGE's co-creators told us flat out that a mother's soul is required for piloting, I'm sure it means nothing.

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Sat Jul 20, 2013 12:37 am I meant to say 'reconfigure the core' , which further gives support to cores not necessarily containing soul of pilots' mothers. Here are my reasons why I think the core doesn't have the soul of mother with exception of Yui in Unit 01. • Rei and Asuka was both considered for the activation of Unit 03, which suggests that they both would have no problem synchronizing with it. Even though actual activation wasn't important since the whole even was a experiment for Seele, no one in Nerv actually knew this meaning Nerve knew that both pilots could activate Unit 03. • It was implied by Gendo and Fuyutsuki that Rei can synchronize with Unit 01. (though this would be possible regardless of soul or not since we now have confirmation of Yui's soul.) • Unit 02 was piloted by Mari. The core was reconfigured into her specification for the synchronization. (One could argue that this is due to her somehow being related to or Mari being some wild card allowing this, but we don't have any clear indication of sorts yet.) I tried to come up with some speculation as to how pilots were shifted around between units if in fact core mechanic was same to that of NGE. Knowing that the cores can be removed and reinstalled from Unit 02's case, could it be that each pilot has their own personal core with their mother's soul? And what they actually reconfigure is not the core but the evangelions in order to accept the personal core of Mari? I can't think of any other way to explain how they can simply switch around, but this 'personalized cores' doesn't hold well either for many reasons. • Mari already blew her core with Provisional Unit before getting on Unit 02. • If Asuka's core was installed on Unit 03 during the activation test, it most likely didn't survive Dummy Plug assault which would contradict the existence of Unit 02 in Q. There is also cases for Mark 9 and Eva 13, but those units have shown some odd and uniqueness to them it's hard to make a case with them at this point. Either way, personalized core idea is no good as well. I honestly think that all other unit cores lack the soul with exception of Yui's case. Assuming the souls do exist in cores, Mari's case with Unit 02 might be because she's some special being that we don't know about yet. But I don't think there is any way to explain the Rei and Asuka for the activation test.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 20, 2013 12:45 am I'm going to be utterly floored if Mari gets any development, be it character or plot, other than "funny big boob glasses girl that likes piloting". This is the Shinji show. Assuming for the moment that the ever-Eva-has-a-soul thing applies to NGE, it would fit thematically if only one Eva has a soul in Rebuild, which is vastly more Shinji-centric.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Sat Jul 20, 2013 7:51 am Bagheera wrote: But you're right about NTE; the whole notion that it has anything to do with the mechanics of NGE is silly.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 20, 2013 8:15 am Yaywalter wrote: It would be silly to say definitively that the "resident soul" aspect of NGE applies to NTE until there's concrete proof, but I fail to see how it's silly to think it's plausible that the mechanic still applies in NTE.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Jul 20, 2013 8:45 am Chuckman wrote: It's handwaving to justify something that isn't in Evangelion anyway. The notion that Every. Single. Eva. has a mother's soul in it is absurd and leads to a bunch of fanwank about Nerv shoving a bunch of women into a soul suckatron or something and storing their souls somehow in case they need a pilot, even though such a program is never mentioned and that technology isn't even implied to exist. The reason the pilot candidates are all motherless is that having no mother makes it easier to synch with an Eva- they're open to the psychological dependence the Eva offers. It's not because there's a giant warehouse full of souls.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Jul 20, 2013 8:54 am I tend to think that the location of the soul in the Evas and Angels is still the core. We see Eva-01 retrieve Rei's soul by pulling Eva-00's core out of Zeruel, and all the Angels turn to red goo after their core is destroyed, which I think the CRC refers to as "image collapse", as in their soul has stopped emitting an ATF to maintain their physical form, similar to tanging. Additionally Eva-01 gets stabbed through the head in 1.0 but still goes beserk immediately afterward, and Eva Mark.09 is apparently fine with losing its head.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Sat Jul 20, 2013 9:25 am Bagheera wrote: The purpose of the mechanic in NGE was to show why the Children had to pilot -- Nerv couldn't just pick anyone to pilot the Evas, because only someone who could sync with the Eva's resident soul was suitable. This obviously isn't in play in NTE; every pilot has piloted multiple Evas, so the resident soul idea serves no purpose in the new setting. Simply put there's no indication in NTE that any Eva apart from Unit 01 has a soul attached to it, and no one watching the show without reference to NGE would suggest such. Why entertain the idea if it serves no narrative or thematic purpose?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 20, 2013 9:40 am Even if the pilots are related (they are, the -nami girls are all clones!) that would require Kyoko's (or equivalent's) soul to be copied, and if they're copies why would there be references to changing core data? Why not just hop in? Fitting the resident soul theory to the movies requires a lot of stretching for no apparent reason. It doesn't appear to matter. Asuka isn't getting anything approaching the complexity of her character arc in the show and Mari is completely superficial, basically eye candy/a sidekick to Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Sat Jul 20, 2013 10:09 am Chuckman wrote: Fitting the resident soul theory to the movies requires a lot of stretching for no apparent reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 20, 2013 10:28 am Yaywalter wrote: It seems to me that the resident soul mechanic, in addition to explaining why the children can only pilot their EVA, is primarily for explaining why the EVAs in general must be piloted by children instead of adults.
summary: []: Core and Pilot Mechanics in NME: Same as NGE?             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Fri Jul 19, 2013 11:12 am [Split from 3.33 Things You Noticed . - Monk] Yaywalter wrote: I was reading the 3.33 faq this evening, and an idea hit me when I read the speculation on the possibility that he isn't Wille's first attempt to recover Shinji due to his foot being labeled "BM-03": How many people were in Unit 01? Three: Shinji
